---
name: status
description: Report the live Synapsys active-domain set. Use when the user says "what domains are active", "synapsys status", "which domains fired", "show active domains", "why did that memory fire", or asks to inspect the live domain classifier. Prints each active root + leaf with signal attribution (which leaf regex matched, or sticky-carry).
argument-hint: '[--session-id=<id>] [--prompt=<text>] [--tool=<ToolName:args>] [--json]'
user-invocable: true
allowed-tools: Bash
---

<!-- codex:plugin-root-preamble v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js; edit the codemod, then re-run -->

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-…}` fallback is NOT safe — validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/status/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/status/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated — use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset — self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls —
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

<!-- codex:arguments-note v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js -->

> **`$ARGUMENTS` under codex**: there is no argument substitution — the
> argument (e.g. the ticket id) is the text after the `$status` skill
> mention in the user's message.

# Status

Run the script. Pass through its output verbatim. The script computes the
active domain set from the registry + sticky state for the current session
and renders attribution per active domain, followed by an **Enforce** section:
memories with `enforce ≠ advise` (level + classifier) and the current
session's `block`/`override` telemetry counts. No agent post-processing.

```bash
node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/synapsys-status.js" $ARGUMENTS
```

Flags (pass through if provided):
- `--session-id=<id>` — session id (defaults to "default")
- `--prompt=<text>` — current prompt text for `signal_prompt` matching
- `--tool=<ToolName:args>` — repeatable; tool calls for `signal_pretool` matching
- `--json` — machine-readable output for piping
- `--no-color` — disable ANSI styling

Fail-open: if `DOMAINS.md` or the sticky-state file are missing/unparseable,
the script prints `no active domains` and exits 0.
